entry.title: How to bypass a 502 Bad Gateway - ResponseWithBody error thrown by the API Gateway?
entry.content:
Hello all,

One of our API proxies is returning an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway - ResponseWithBody response. This is due to the API Gateway considering the response of the API provider incorrect, as it contains an "Content-Encoding" header along with an HTTP 204 response code, which is not a correct HTTP response according to the RFC 7231, section 6.3.5: 204 No Content.

Because the fix on API provider side will take several months to be deployed, we would like to temporarily bypass the error and "force" the response received by the API provider being sent back to the API consumer.

We have tried different solutions without success:

  • Removing the problematic HTTP header from the response using an AssignMessage policy in the response PreFflow of the target endpoint.
  • Forcing the response code to 204 in an AssignMessage policy called from the <FaultRules> section of both the proxy and target endpoints
  • By calling the API provider using a service callout instead of a target endpoint.

Unfortunately, none of these solutions works, and the HTTP 502 Bad Gateway - ResponseWithBody is still being returned by the API proxy.

Is it possible to bypass this error and force the reponse to a specific HTTP code ?

Thanks

entry.title: IDES ECC6 EHP8 working - ADS adobe / JAVA STACK issue
entry.content:
Hello,
I own an IDES ECC6 EHP8 home server for my personal use and for personal training purposes (mainly abap training).


This server is a VMWARE image. Everything works perfectly, the only problem I have is that ADS doesn't work. When I want to learn adobe forms I have a problem because I can't activate my form and when I want to preview print I get an error message “soap runtime exception: csoapexceptiontransport : http receive failed with exception communicati on_failure (100.101).”

After my research someone told that this is an ADS and java stack configuration error and that I need to configure an additional java server to connect the ADS services.

I don't know anything about this, as it's a BASIS subject for which I have no expertise. Could someone help me via Anydesk or Teamviewer? I can pay for the service because I'm a junior and I really need it to improve my skills (I should point out that my consulting company doesn't have a sandbox server, unfortunately).

entry.title: Re: Comparison of Users from SuccessFactors(odata) and Custom MDF using SAP CPI/CI
entry.content:
As an alternative solution, we can use the steps below to achieve the results:
Step 1: Combine both input payloads into a single message body through a content modifier:
<Root>
<SF_Data>
${property.SF_payload}
</SF_Data>
<MDF_Data>
${property.MDF_payload}
</MDF_Data>
</Root>
Step 2: Use the XSLT code below to achieve the desired output:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">
<xsl:output method="xml" indent="yes" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"/>
<xsl:template match="/">
<RemovedUsers>
<xsl:for-each select="Root/SF_Data/JobRequisition/JobRequisitionOperator">
<xsl:variable name="currentUser" select="usersSysId"/>
<xsl:if test="not(//MDF_Data/JobRequisition/JobRequisitionOperator[usersSysId = $currentUser])">
<RemovedUser><xsl:value-of select="$currentUser"/></RemovedUser>
</xsl:if>
</xsl:for-each>
</RemovedUsers>
</xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

entry.title: FI Document Clearing
entry.content:
Dear experts hi,

My requirement is to get 2 or more existing open items of a customer and clear them using ABAP and the result to be a journal entry with no lines.

I have tried the POSTING_INTERFACE_CLEARING but this gets stuck in the Process Open Items screen. Doing some debugging i noticed that there isn't any step to activate the items and the FM expects to enter data in structure t_ftpost, but this is not my request since doing this will create another two lines debiting & crediting.

I am now trying to use AC_CLR_DOCUMENT_CREATE. I have managed to clear the documents but the generated document has a single line with zero amount either a customer or GL depending on how you feed the FM.

This is how the FM is filled:

ls_acchd-glvor = 'RFBU'.
ls_acchd-awtyp = 'BKPF'.
ls_acchd-tcode = 'FB1D'.
APPEND ls_acchd TO it_acchd.
CLEAR: ls_acchd.

g_posnr = 1.
ls_accit-bukrs = bukrs.
ls_accit-budat = sy-datum.
ls_accit-bldat = sy-datum.
ls_accit-wwert = sy-datum.
ls_accit-blart = 'SA'.
ls_accit-posnr = g_posnr.
ls_accit-bschl = '50'.
ls_accit-koart = 'S'.
ls_accit-hkont = gl_account.  
*  ls_accit-kunnr = kunnr.
*  ls_accit-bschl = '01'.
*  ls_accit-koart = 'D'.
ls_accit-pswsl = 'EUR'.
APPEND ls_accit TO it_accit.

ls_acccr-posnr = g_posnr.
ls_acccr-waers = 'EUR'.
ls_acccr-curtp = '00'.
APPEND ls_acccr TO it_acccr.

ls_acccr-posnr = g_posnr.
ls_acccr-waers = 'EUR'.
ls_acccr-curtp = '10'.
APPEND ls_acccr TO it_acccr.

LOOP AT it_documents INTO ls_lines.
  ls_accclr_itm_key-bukrs = bukrs.
  ls_accclr_itm_key-koart = 'D'.
  ls_accclr_itm_key-konko = kunnr.
  ls_accclr_itm_key-belnr = ls_lines-belnr.
  ls_accclr_itm_key-gjahr = ls_lines-gjahr.
  ls_accclr_itm_key-buzei = ls_lines-buzei.
  APPEND ls_accclr_itm_key TO it_accclr_itm_key.

  g_posnr = g_posnr + 1.
  CLEAR: ls_lines,ls_accit,ls_accclr_itm_key,ls_bseg.
ENDLOOP.
  CALL FUNCTION 'AC_CLR_DOCUMENT_CREATE'
    EXPORTING
      i_auglv                = 'EINGZAHL'
      i_koart                = 'D'
      i_bukrs                = bukrs
      i_konko                = kunnr
    TABLES
      t_acchd                = it_acchd
      t_accit                = it_accit
      t_acccr                = it_acccr
      t_accclr_itm_key       = it_accclr_itm_key
    EXCEPTIONS
      invalid_input          = 1
      invalid_selection      = 2
      account_missing        = 3
      account_not_oi_handled = 4
      account_locked         = 5
      document_locked        = 6
      account_clr_internally = 7
      OTHERS                 = 8.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
  ENDIF.

Do you know if there is any other solution for this? Batch Input is not an option.

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: Convert Sapi Source system to ODP source system
entry.content:
Yes but only whole dataflows can be converted. This means, that cubes and classic DSO-s are converted to ADSO-s. Multiproviders, Infosets are converted to HCPR and so on.

But you can also try the following:

  1. Create the new ODP source system.
  2. Put a single datasource of the SAPI source system on a transport.
  3. In RSLOGSYSMAP map the SAPI source system to the new ODP source system.
  4. Import the transport created in step 2 back into the same system
  5. If successful the datasource will be imported to the new ODP source system

Note that the existing deltas will not work with the newly created ODP datasources. They need to be newly initialized.

entry.title: Re: Finding the right CDS Extractor in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Jens, unfortunately, the CDS extractors do not read from achives generated by tr. SARA. The data needs to be available on the HANA platform, however SARA generates offline archives.  Same applied for S-API extractors in the past. 
Regards - Frank

entry.title: Re: Download path of Payment Media in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Felix,

So far, I didn't get any good solution for this unfortunately.

Basically, file download from Fiori apps triggers browser download, so file path depends on each user's browser setting.

In some cases, extension using scope item "2YM" could be a solution (while this item requires Cash Management license).
This scope item activates an API which passes payment media files to middleware via SOAP.
This solution could be adopted if the costs of these additional license and extension are accepted.

Best regards,
Tomoyoshi

The only available BAdI (ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST) does not stop the program when attempting to check these indicators.

i'll show below step by step.
1) We add one material 

2) press "Save"

3) then my BADI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST is activated.

thanks in advance for your collab,

G. & E.

entry.title: Re: XSLT Transformation and standard text for ROUTING_CREATE
entry.content:
EDI_DATA_INCOMING is to process an input file, either in text or XML format depending on the port passed as parameter. In XML format, it should be something like that:

<IDOC BEGIN="1">
    <EDI_DC40 SEGMENT="1">
        <TABNAM>EDI_DC40</TABNAM>
        <IDOCTYP>DELVRY03</IDOCTYP>
        <MESTYP>DESADV</MESTYP>
        <SNDPOR>whatever</SNDPOR>
        <SNDPRT>LS</SNDPRT>
        <SNDPRN>PARTNERWE20</SNDPRN>
        <RCVPRT>whatever</RCVPRT>
        <RCVPRN>whatever</RCVPRN>
    </EDI_DC40>
    <E1BP1012_TXT_HDR_C SEGMENT="1">
        <OBJECT_TYPE>BE</OBJECT_TYPE>
        ...

So, the XSLT transformation must convert from the input XML to the above IDoc XML (there are lots of examples and resources everywhere, it should not very complex), you'll get an XML with the text expressed as one big string, so after the transformation, you'll have to do ABAP code to transform again the big string into an internal table inside the XML.

The ABAP would look like this (there are probably many errors, but you've got the big picture):

CALL TRANSFORMATION zmaterial_routing_to_idoc
  SOURCE XML material_routing
  RESULT XML material_routing_idoc.

FIND REGEX '(<PSEUDO_BAPI1012_TXT_C>)(.*)(</PSEUDO_BAPI1012_TXT_C>)' 
    IN material_routing_idoc 
    SUBMATCHES 
      DATA(begin) 
      DATA(XML_PSEUDO_BAPI1012_TXT_C)
      DATA(end).

IF sy-subrc = 0.

  DATA(PSEUDO_BAPI1012_TXT_C) = VALUE string( ).
  CALL TRANSFORMATION ID SOURCE XML XML_PSEUDO_BAPI1012_TXT_C
                         RESULT XML PSEUDO_BAPI1012_TXT_C.

  DATA(stream_lines_BAPI1012_TXT_C) = VALUE string_table(
                     ( PSEUDO_BAPI1012_TXT_C ) ).
  DATA(itf_text) = VALUE tline_tab( ).
  CALL FUNCTION 'CONVERT_STREAM_TO_ITF_TEXT'
    EXPORTING
      stream_lines = stream_lines_BAPI1012_TXT_C
      lf           = 'X'
    IMPORTING
      itf_text     = itf_text.

  DATA(BAPI1012_TXT_C) = CONV TT_BAPI1012_TXT_C( itf_text ).

  DATA(XML_BAPI1012_TXT_C) = VALUE string( ).
  CALL TRANSFORMATION ID SOURCE DATA = BAPI1012_TXT_C
                         RESULT XML  XML_BAPI1012_TXT_C
                         OPTIONS xml_header = 'no'.

  REPLACE REGEX '<DATA>(.*)</DATA>' IN XML_BAPI1012_TXT_C WITH '$1'.

  REPLACE |{ begin }{ XML_PSEUDO_BAPI1012_TXT_C }{ end }| 
          IN material_routing_idoc 
          WITH |{ begin }{ XML_BAPI1012_TXT_C }{ end }|.
ENDIF.

entry.content: I agree concerning the data element. What is the "display only" box? Note that selecting all the checkboxes is not the issue.

entry.title: Re: ME21N CHECK ON WARNING RED-LIGHTS NEEDS TO STOP THE P...
entry.content:
Hi Dominik,

This is what the client has requested:
At the moment of "Save," if there are any red traffic lights, the program must not proceed with posting and should stop before the "Hold" step in the pop-up.

entry.title: SAP CDS "The entity 'xyz' requires parameter 'yyy'"
entry.content:
Hi there, this is my first post - please ignore if this is not relevant.

We have a business requirement to extract data to be used in SAP Analysis for Office by using CDS view 

'IFIFINSTMTCUDE' with additional fields from a custom table function whereby the level text from FAGL_011QT needs to be displayed.

The custom table extract on it's own display the data as expected, but in the custom CDS , the message 'The entity zfi_financial_level requires parameter z_vonkt' .

1. is it possible to do what I try to do ?

2. if it is not possible, is there anyone with an idea how to do it? I am stuck 🙂

3. I know there is still a bit of development to do, but for testing, I need to be able to activate the code

CDS copy and changed:

@EndUserText.label: 'Financial Statement Cube View'

@ObjectModel.usageType.sizeCategory: #XXL
@ObjectModel.usageType.serviceQuality: #D

@ObjectModel.modelingPattern: #ANALYTICAL_QUERY

-- Time Parameters

-- Time Comparison Parameters
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

type : #CONSTANT,
value : 'X'
}
]

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

}

association [0..1] to zfi_financial_level as zfl on zfl.vonkt = I_FinancialStatementCube.GLAccount

zfl.Level1,
zfl.Level2,
zfl.Level3,
zfl.Level4,
zfl.Level5
}

 

Table function:

@EndUserText.label: 'ZFI Financial Level'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
define table function zfi_financial_level
with parameters z_vonkt: vonkt_011z
---- @Environment.systemField: #CLIENT clnt : abap.clnt--,

returns
{
clnt : abap.clnt;
vonkt : vonkt_011z;

Level1 : ztxt45_lvl1;
Level2 : ztxt45_lvl2;
Level3 : ztxt45_lvl3;
Level4 : ztxt45_lvl4;
Level5 : ztxt45_lvl5;
}
implemented by method
zcl_fi_stmnt_lvl=>get_level_txt;

 

Table method:

class zcl_fi_stmnt_lvl definition

public

final

create public .

 

public section.

 

interfaces if_amdp_marker_hdb.

 

class-methods get_level_txt

for table function zfi_financial_level.

 

protected section.

private section.

endclass.

 

 

class zcl_fi_stmnt_lvl implementation.

 

method get_level_txt by database function for hdb

language sqlscript

options read-only

using fagl_011zc

fagl_011pc

fagl_011qt.

 

declare i integer;

 

declare temp1 varchar( 50 );

declare temp2 varchar( 50 );

declare temp3 varchar( 50 );

declare temp4 varchar( 50 );

declare temp5 varchar( 50 );

declare counter integer;

declare counter_to integer;

 

/* structure to return */

 

declare return_table table ( clnt varchar(3),

vonkt varchar( 20 ),

Level1 varchar( 45 ),

Level2 varchar( 45 ),

Level3 varchar( 45 ),

Level4 varchar( 45 ),

Level5 varchar( 45 ) );

 

lt_ret =

select distinct zc.mandt as clnt,

zc.vonkt as vonkt,

pc.ergsl as ergsl1,

t1.txt45 as Level1,

pc2.ergsl as ergsl2,

t2.txt45 as Level2,

pc3.ergsl as ergsl3,

t3.txt45 as Level3,

pc4.ergsl as ergsl4,

t4.txt45 as Level4,

pc5.ergsl as ergsl5,

t5.txt45 as Level5

 

from fagl_011zc as zc

 

--1

left outer join fagl_011pc as pc

on pc.versn = zc.versn

and pc.ergsl = zc.ergsl

-- and pc.mandt = clnt

left join fagl_011qt as t1

on t1.versn = zc.versn

and t1.spras = 'E'

and t1.ergsl = pc.ergsl

-- and t1.mandt = clnt

 

--2

left outer join fagl_011pc as pc2

on pc2.versn = zc.versn

and pc2.id = pc.parent

-- and pc2.mandt = clnt

 

left join fagl_011qt as t2

on t2.versn = zc.versn

and t2.spras = 'E'

and t2.ergsl = pc2.ergsl

-- and t2.mandt = clnt

 

--3

left outer join fagl_011pc as pc3

on pc3.versn = zc.versn

and pc3.id = pc2.parent

-- and pc3.mandt = clnt

 

left join fagl_011qt as t3

on t3.versn = zc.versn

and t3.spras = 'E'

and t3.ergsl = pc3.ergsl

-- and t3.mandt = clnt

 

--4

left outer join fagl_011pc as pc4

on pc4.versn = zc.versn

and pc4.id = pc3.parent

-- and pc4.mandt = clnt

 

left join fagl_011qt as t4

on t4.versn = zc.versn

and t4.spras = 'E'

and t4.ergsl = pc4.ergsl

-- and t4.mandt = clnt

 

--5

left outer join fagl_011pc as pc5

on pc5.versn = zc.versn

and pc5.id = pc4.parent

-- and pc5.mandt = clnt

 

left join fagl_011qt as t5

on t5.versn = zc.versn

and t5.spras = 'E'

and t5.ergsl = pc5.ergsl

-- and t5.mandt = clnt

-- :clnt, :z_versn, :z_vonkt are all input parameters

where --zc.mandt = clnt

--and

zc.versn = 'NPLC' --:z_versn

and zc.ktopl = 'NPL1'

 

 

and zc.vonkt = :z_vonkt -- added 20241114 again for testing -- added 20241114 for testing

 

-- 20241115

group by

zc.mandt,

zc.vonkt, --as vonkt,

pc.ergsl, -- as ergsl1,

t1.txt45, -- as Level1,

pc2.ergsl, -- as ergsl2,

t2.txt45, -- as Level2,

pc3.ergsl, -- as ergsl3,

t3.txt45, -- as Level3,

pc4.ergsl, -- as ergsl4,

t4.txt45, -- as Level4,

pc5.ergsl, -- as ergsl5,

t5.txt45 --as Level5

-- 20241115

 

order by vonkt, Level1

;

 

i = 1;

 

for i in 1 .. 1 do --RECORD_COUNT(:lt_ret) do

temp1 := ' ';

temp2 := ' ';

temp3 := ' ';

temp4 := ' ';

temp5 := ' ';

 

if :lt_ret.Level5[ i ] is not null

then

temp1 = :lt_ret.Level5[ i ];

temp2 = :lt_ret.Level4[ i ];

temp3 = :lt_ret.Level3[ i ];

temp4 = :lt_ret.Level2[ i ];

temp5 = :lt_ret.Level1[ i ];

 

else

if :lt_ret.Level4[ i ] is not null

then

temp1 := :lt_ret.Level4[ i ];

temp2 := :lt_ret.Level3[ i ];

temp3 := :lt_ret.Level2[ i ];

temp4 := :lt_ret.Level1[ i ];

 

if :lt_ret.Level3[ i ] is not null

then

temp1 := :lt_ret.Level3[ i ];

temp2 := :lt_ret.Level2[ i ];

temp3 := :lt_ret.Level1[ i ];

 

if :lt_ret.Level2[ i ] is not null

then

temp1 := :lt_ret.Level2[ i ];

temp2 := :lt_ret.Level1[ i ];

else

temp1 := :lt_ret.Level1[ i ];

end if;

end if;

 

end if;

 

end if;

 

lt_ret.Level1[ i ] := :temp1;

lt_ret.Level2[ i ] := :temp2;

lt_ret.Level3[ i ] := :temp3;

lt_ret.Level4[ i ] := :temp4;

lt_ret.Level5[ i ] := :temp5;

 

return_table.vonkt[ 1 ] = :lt_ret.vonkt[ 1 ];

return_table.Level1[ 1 ] = :lt_ret.Level1[ 1 ];

return_table.Level2[ 1 ] = :lt_ret.Level2[ 1 ];

return_table.Level3[ 1 ] = :lt_ret.Level3[ 1 ];

return_table.Level4[ 1 ] = :lt_ret.Level4[ 1 ];

return_table.Level5[ 1 ] = :lt_ret.Level5[ 1 ];

end for;

 

-- return :lt_ret;

return :return_table;

endmethod.

endclass.

entry.title: Re: Auto layout to beautify Integration Flow in the Cloud Integration capability of SAP Integration
entry.content: Is there any solution in the meantime? Same problem on my side.

entry.title: Re: Packaging Material not relevant for Billing S4Hana Cloud
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have solved the issue. HUBM does not exist in the copying control from Delivery item to Invoice. HUBP does.

We are using HUBP item category for the packaging materials and logic to make the item category not relevant for billing. 

Kind regards, 

Randy

entry.title: CALM & MS Teams Integration changes due to MS O365 connector “retirement”
entry.content:
Due to Microsoft announcement the Office 365 connectors will be retired in the future and the new way to publish notifications into TEAMS channels is to use “MS Workflows” instead.

SAP already updated their documentation for CALM to configure the integration with MS Teams channels.
As far as I understand the documentation SAP changes the implementation. Instead of using one destination to MS Teams it is now required to setup one destination for each channel.
Am I right and is SAP going to support “Workflows” in “Webhooks & Subscriptions Management” in the future?

entry.title: Get insights into the 2H2024 release highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content:
Our future-ready workforce product strategy has a strong emphasis on engaging experiences, AI and skills. It aims to establish the talent intelligence hub (TIH) as the single source of truth for skills across SAP SuccessFactors solutions including SAP SuccessFactors Learning to use AI to continuously help organizations and employees understand their skills and match people with personalized career pathways and learning opportunities. This strategy is advancing, delivering new capabilities across the talent suite and facilitating strategic investments. As recently announced at SuccessConnect, the talent intelligence hub now integrates skills data from external partners..

Partners such as Beamery, Degreed, Korn Ferry, Lightcast, Skillsoft, TalenTeam, and TechWolf are integrating, or preparing to integrate, their skills data into the talent intelligence hub. This integration enables organizations to incorporate existing skills frameworks and create a unified, future-ready workforce—with more integrations and partners planned for the future. New AI and skills-related capabilities play a significant role in the 2H2024 release for SAP SuccessFactors, including enhancements for Learning. Additionally, the 2H 2024 release introduces several exciting innovations for Learning, including:

Enhancements for the integrated learning experience (ILX)

Over the past several releases, we've introduced and enhanced the Integrated Learning Experience (ILX) for learners, providing clarity and prioritization for what the user 'must, should, and wants to learn'. We continue to improve the learner discovery experience through the addition of custom pages and category browsing capabilities. Utilizing the card and banner templates from ILX, customers can now create custom pages that seamlessly integrate with the existing ILX, making personalization and branding straightforward for administrators to deploy to your users. Curating additional learning experiences for your users that showcase initiatives, highlight important learning topics, and lead learners to a more engaging path of discovery helps to drive organizational specific needs and to guide them along their learning journey. This added flexibility has been highly requested by our customers using ILX.

Approval cards are now added to the new learning experience, following the same pattern established on the SAP SuccessFactors home page which is a key function for managers and other users who are regularly tasked with approving training requests. ​ Users encounter various learning scenarios, based on administrator configurations, that require approval requests to proceed. For example, learners may need approval to enroll in a course or class, or to withdraw from a class. ​In some cases, learners and managers request approval when they must add learning to the history.​​ Users now defined with one of default roles as approvers, such as line managers, second level managers, instructors or HR business partners will now be able to see these approvals directly on the new ILX.

We’ve also introduced a delegate experience: When a user has been assigned as a delegate, they can act on behalf of the delegator from the new home page. The access of a delegator's new homepage is based on permissions given. In delegate mode, the delegate will see a message strip on all pages, clearly indicating they are acting on behalf of the delegator. To return to their own homepage, delegates can simply click "Return to My Learning."

We also provide additional insights to learners when they are engaging with recommended content. The source of recommendation (admin, peer, personalized) can be shown to help learners understand who made the recommendation and encourages them to complete it. Learners can also access external links recommended by administrators or peers on the new homepage.

There are much more enhancements delivered for ILX than the ones above. Please note that the option to disable the Integrated Learning Experience got removed to progress towards the deprecation of the legacy experience in a future release. But let’s switch gears now from the learner to the manager.

New manager view

After enhancing the learner experience in the first two releases of the new ILX and introducing significant improvements to the administrator experience—such as new configuration options for custom cards and banners in 1H 2024—we're excited to unveil a refreshed manager view, that ties to the new design of the new learning home page. The new manager view shows the manager’s direct reports and gives an overview of the status of their team members’ current assignments, reflecting the categories of required learning, important learning, self-driven learning, while enabling them to take care about next measures.

In the refreshed manager view, managers can take action for individuals, such as view or add to their learning history, manage or request observers, mark requirements as complete, review an item’s prerequisites or curriculum details, and register them to a class. They can also send each person a reminder to complete their overdue assignments.

 

Managers can also take bulk actions for their team, such as nudge multiple direct reports in one go, which will send them all an email reminder to complete their overdue training in just a few clicks.

 

At our SuccessConnect event we just announced that 30 new embedded AI use cases across the HCM suite are going to get delivered with 2H2024 release to help provide organizations with greater efficiencies and experiences. Let’s focus on some which are tightly related to Learning.

AI-capabilities

Customers can now import predefined skills provided by Open Content Network (OCN) partners. Those providers can import the associated skills along with courses into SAP SuccessFactors Learning. The skills are automatically standardized with skills from talent intelligence hub (TIH) and associated to Learning courses.  This feature allows customers to seamlessly integrate the skills configured in the talent intelligence hub (TIH) with Learning, ensuring consistency and alignment. As a result, administrators can boost productivity by eliminating the need to manually associate skills with OCN courses.

 AI capabilities can also now be used to extract skills from item descriptions to align the skills with the talent intelligence hub’s attributes library and to automatically associate them with learning items. This feature helps to infer skills for courses in bulk, allowing customers to automatically apply the skills configured in the talent intelligence hub (TIH) within Learning. This helps to eliminate the need to manually identify and assign skills, streamlining the process.

We now offer an AI-assisted writing tool that uses generative AI capabilities to assist with writing tasks. The interactive tool can be leveraged to enhance multiple aspects of writing such as clarity or conciseness. In 2H 2024, the writing assistant will be available in the title & description fields for items, curricula, programs, collections, and quick guides.

 

 Looking ahead to early 2025, we're excited to introduce even more AI-powered capabilities for Learning. These include Joule integration in Learning, which will enable users to leverage conversational AI to find learning content and check team compliance status, as well as an intelligent Q&A tool that provides instant answers to questions about your SAP SuccessFactors Learning content. This tool will pull resources and reference links from a variety of sources, including courses, videos, and documents across different standards. But we'll save the details for our next release blog!

 

Are you interested in more information on the 2H2024 release highlights? Then check out the SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2024 Release Highlights Learning video,  Release Highlights Brochure  Release Highlights Video and explore more Release Information at our customer community. You can also find a list of all enhancements on the What’s New Viewer and leverage the Roadmap Explorer to learn more about what’s planned for the future!

Please share the exciting news within the Learning community about some of the highlights to come.

Margit Bauer, Director Product Marketing for SAP SuccessFactors Learning

entry.title: Re: SAP analytics cloud - How can i filter a story based...
entry.content:
Hi A.K,

One easy way to achieve this is 

1.Created a hidden table which filtered based on user logged in i got from your script function

var loggedInUser = Application.getUserInfo().displayName;

Once this hidden table is filtered you will get 'Department' member which he belongs with this function

var rslt =tab_hidden.getDataSource().getResultSet();
var dept=rslt[0]["Dept"].id;

2.Cretae a input control to filter all relevant widgets in your story with below function

IC_1.getInputControlDataSource().setSelectedMembers(dept);

With this it will filter the story with dept that user is logged in.

 

There are other ways too but i feel this is easy and simple to restrict widgets in story with the required department.

Thanks,

Venkat

Your custom field I can see is associated with yesNo picklist which has two values, hence making the custom field a boolean values field.

entry.title: SCIM API Filter is throwing an error when using SAP SuccessFactors schema extensions
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We want to change the userName attribute for the user in SAP SuccessFactors and want to use the SCIM API for this.
Between SAP SuccessFactors and our IAM, we have SAP Cloud Identity Services as a proxy.
The whole thing also works as long as we use the user ID which uses the SCIM API directly in the URL.

We don't have and don't want to keep this ID in the IAM, as it is something internal to SF and CIS.

With the SCIM API, it is also possible to query other attributes to read out a specific user.
This is done via the URL parameter "filter".

So we can read out a user via SCIM by going to the attribute “personIdExternal”.
This also works if the SCIM API end point is SAP SuccessFactors.

 

GET { 
url: https://<sf-data-center-tenant>/rest/iam/scim/v2/Users?filter=urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:successfactors:2.0:User:personIdExternal+eq+"1234567892"
body: json
auth: bearer
}

 

As a result, I get the user who has this “personIdExternal”.

As I have already mentioned, we still have SAP Cloud Identity Services between IAM and SAP SuccessFactors, which acts as a proxy

Unfortunately, this doesn't work.
The SCIM API end point looks like this:

 

GET { 
 url: https://xxxxxxxxxx.accounts.cloud.sap/ipsproxy/service/api/v1/scim/<sf-tenant>/Users?filter=urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:successfactors:2.0:User:personIdExternal+eq+"1234567892"
body: json
auth: basic
}

 

Here I get the following error:

 

{
  "schemas": [
    "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"
  ],
  "detail": "Invalid filter value.",
  "status": 422
}

 

 From my point of view this should work or is this unsupported by SAP CIS?

Thanks for your tips and help 😄

best regards,
Andy

entry.title: Where can I learn mor about change management methodology an activities?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Acting as Professional Change Manager in SAP Cloud Projects learning journey.


Overview
This learning journey will enable the learner to apply organizational change management in an SAP cloud project to support the transformation process towards a high and sustainable user adoption and an accelerated adoption of innovation.

Learning objectives
After the completion of this learning journey, project managers and consultants will have developed an in-depth understanding of SAP’s organizational change management methodology. They will be able to plan and execute change management activities to professionally handle the people side of cloud deployments during all SAP Activate phases. In addition, they will be aware of the success factors for an effective change management delivery.

Prerequisites

  • Basic knowledge of project management, preferably in the context of SAP implementation projects.
  • Completion of the learning journey
    "Discover Organizational Change Management for SAP Cloud Projects".

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Unicode Conversion Issue - ABAP, modification in database table.
entry.content:
Facing unicode conversion issue in modify statement.

 

Here's the code

DATA: datum TYPE sy-datum.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-001.
PARAMETERS: p_date TYPE datum.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

CONSTANTS:lc_tabname TYPE tabname VALUE 'ztfre_layout'.

IF p_date IS INITIAL.
p_date = sy-datum - 1.
ENDIF.

IF p_date IS NOT INITIAL.

SELECT min_stock,
zvalto
FROM ztfre_layout
WHERE ( min_stock IS NOT INITIAL AND zvalto LT @p_date )
OR min_stock IS INITIAL
INTO TABLE @DATA(layout_tab).
IF sy-subrc = 0.

LOOP AT layout_tab ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<layout>).
<layout>-min_stock = 0.
ENDLOOP.


IF layout_tab IS NOT INITIAL.

CALL FUNCTION 'ENQUEUE_E_TABLE'
EXPORTING
mode_rstable = 'E'
tabname = lc_tabname
EXCEPTIONS
foreign_lock = 1
system_failure = 2
OTHERS = 3.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
MODIFY ztfre_layout FROM TABLE layout_tab.
MESSAGE: 'Records updated successfully' TYPE 'S'.
COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

CALL FUNCTION 'DEQUEUE_E_TABLE'
EXPORTING
tabname = lc_tabname.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

ENDIF.
ENDIF.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, November 14th, 2024
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Developing Event-Driven Integrations with SAP BTP 📖

  • SAP Press E-Bite: https://www.sap-press.com/developing-event-driven-integrations-with-sap-btp_6021/
  • GitHub repository: https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-e-bite/

Terraform Provider Updates

  • Terraform Exporter for SAP BTP https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/may-i-introduce-you-terraform-exporter-sap-btp-christian-lechner-lgzif/
  • Terraform Provider for Cloud Foundry 1.1.0 https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/terraform-provider-cloud-foundry-v110-whats-new-debaditya-ray-xyxyc/

Report from SAP Insider EMEA 2024

  • SAP Insider EMEA 2024 event https://reg.eventmobi.com/emea-2024
  • UKISUG Connect 2024 event https://www.ukisugconnect.co.uk/
  • SAUG-x event https://www.saug.com.au/WhatsOn/SAUG-x2024

Report from ASUG Tech Connect

  • https://www.asug.com/events/asug-tech-connect-2024

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

00:07 Report from ASUG Tech Connect

02:47 Report from SAP Insider EMEA 2024

04:10 Developing Event-Driven Integrations with SAP BTP 📖

05:04 Terraform Provider Updates

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for November 14th, 2024.

[Devtoberfest Winners] Hi, I'm Bato. I'm one of the Devtoberfest winners of last year. I'm here at the ASUG and enjoying a conference, doing hands-on labs, doing interesting sessions from SAP and some partners and enjoying the time. Hi, my name is Sebastian. I'm coming from Germany and I'm one of the Devtoberfest winner from 2023. It's my pleasure to be here. It's very nice TechEd conference and I love it to contact a lot of other people and to have fun. My name is Daniel also winner of this year's Devtoberfest. I'm very excited to be here. We learned a lot of new things and I'm also very excited for the disney film tomorrow. Hello, my name is Jarosław Piotrko, I won the Devtoberfest 2023. I would like to greet all programmers from Poland and encourage you to participate in Devtoberfest, because you always have a chance to win and participate in such a fantastic event, and in the journey to the Wolt Adysteia Park. Thank you. Hello, I am Abul Eshtam, the winner of Devtoberfest 2023. Here I am at SAP TechConnect attending this session on AI and different solutions of SAP and about knowing about SAP Joule. A big thanks to SAP Developer Advocate for this opportunity. Thank you. Hi, I am Sandra, I am from Mexico, and I am a winner of Devtoberfest  2023. So, hi developers, I am here in Teques, finally I met the developer advocates and I have learned a lot on the hands-on sessions. See you! Hola, yo soy Sandra de Mexico y soy una de las ganadoras del Devtoberfest 2023. Hi Bablock, I'm finally in SAP TechEd and I met the developers advocates and I've also learned a lot about hands-on. I'll tell you later. See you!

[DJ] Good morning, I'm here in Copenhagen, day three, the morning of day three, the last day of the SAP Insider EMEA 2024 event. As you can see, there's a little bit of a great time happening right now. I'm here in the LEGO area, what's not to like, but basically, it's been a great event. This is one of a number of events that's been happening post SAP TechEd Virtual 2024. We've had three days, well, two and a half days so far, all fantastic conversation, face-to-face conversation, talking about tech, getting to know each other, and also learning from each other in various sessions, lectures, but also hands-on sessions, two-hour hands-on sessions that have been very successful and very well received. We've also had a developer garage staffed by the developer advocates just down there. But anyway, it's been a great event. Look out for more events coming your way. There's one in Birmingham in the UK in early December as part of the UKISUG Connect and also one in Australia coming up next week, I think. Anyway, it's me, DJ, signing off for now from SAP Insider EMEA 2024. Cool.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. Are you interested in learning about event-driven integrations? Well, I've got some news to share with you. Check out the new SAP Press eByte Developing Event-Driven Integrations with SAP BTP. It was released this week. Event-driven integrations are all about systems communicating in real time, reacting to changes instantly, and creating seamless experience for our customers. In the invite, I dive deep into how you can leverage SAP BTP to build event-driven integrations with the different offerings available. There is also an accompanying GitHub repo with code samples and integration artifacts to help you get started. It is a great time to explore how event-driven architectures can transform your integration strategy. I'm including the links in the description. Enjoy.

[DJ] In the infrastructures code arena, Terraform is where it's at and the Terraform Provider for SAP BTP released last year is already a really successful piece of software. This week a new part of the Terraform Provider for SAP BTP was released, the Terraform Exporter for SAP BTP. Now this is a result of lots of customer feedback, the number one wish is to of course be able to start with existing resources on SAP BTP and bring them into the Terraform home. This is a beta release of the Terraform exporter for SAP BTP, there's plenty of information about it from Christian's blog post, the documentation on the GitHub repo and there's also a video. So why not give it a try and let the team know what you think, they're eager to find out. And talking of Terraform, the latest release of the newly written Cloud Foundry provider, the Terraform provider for Cloud Foundry is out 1.1.0. This release brings lots of new features and improvements, so check out the blog post and give that a try as well. Just if you're wondering, just as the BTP CLI is for managing resources on SAP BTP on the command line, and the CF CLI is for managing resources in Cloud Foundry specifically on the command line, so is the Terraform provider for SAP PTP and the Terraform provider for Cloud Foundry. They sort of parallel each other. Anyway, give it a go, and again, let the team know what you think.
